spec_match.rationale: Chromium-catches-up feature — the spec already documents the behaviour, so the value of the demo is the interop story. The concept exercises one corner but the before/after comparison isn't always crisp.

interactive.rationale: A static policy selector and simulate button now drive the same live SVG link, referrer flow, security band, and SVG/HTML comparison table as the generated policy buttons.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Either the feature-index blurb or the concept lede is missing — can't verify alignment.

edge_cases.rationale: Some fallback / error handling is present but corner cases like removed-element behaviour, denied permission flow, or no-support browsers aren't all covered.

references.rationale: Page includes the chromestatus.com/feature/5140707648077824 link in the see-also section.

design_system.rationale: The generated SVG now reads colours from shared CSS variables, and the new controls inherit the global button color pair.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: each attribute exercised independently
openQuestions.detail: The support-ping-hreflang-type-and-referrerpolicy-for-svgaelement feature's headline framing — SVGAElement gains ping / hreflang / type / referrerpolicy attributes (already on HTMLAnchorElement) — calls out "each attribute exercised independently". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: side-by-side with the HTML <a> equivalent
openQuestions.detail: The support-ping-hreflang-type-and-referrerpolicy-for-svgaelement feature's headline framing — SVGAElement gains ping / hreflang / type / referrerpolicy attributes (already on HTMLAnchorElement) — calls out "side-by-side with the HTML <a> equivalent". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

openQuestions.title: Align the concept lede with the feature-index blurb
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index sells one promise; the concept lede frames a different angle. Either reword the lede to echo the blurb's keywords (so visitors recognise they're in the right place) or update the parent blurb.

summary: security-policy under support-ping-hreflang-type-and-referrerpolicy-for-svgaelement: 3 pass / 3 partial / 0 fail. Static policy controls and CSS-variable SVG colours resolve the major interaction gap and design drift; broader attribute coverage remains.
